sections.paragraph:
-

(1) prop. to chastise (Arab. لد i.q. لدم to strike, to beat with a rod), especially beasts of burden (whence מַלְמָד an ox-goad); hence to discipline, to train cattle (see Pual, Hos 10:11), recruits for war; 1Ch 15:18, לְמוּדֵי מִלְחָמָה “trained (i.e. practised) for war;” compare Pual No. 2.

(2) Intrans. to be trained, to learn, e.g. war, Isa 2:4 to accustom oneself to any thing, followed by אֶל Jer 10:2 by an infinitive, Isa 1:17 a gerund, Deu 14:23 17:19 18:9 followed by an acc. Deu 5:1 Isa 26:10.

Piel למַּד.-

(1) to train, to accustom; Jer 9:4, “they have accustomed their tongue to speak lies.”

(2) to teach; const.-(a) absol. 2Ch 17:7.-(b) followed by an acc. of pers. to teach any one something, Psa 71:17 Son 8:2.-(c) followed by two acc. of pers. and thing, Deu 4:1, , 14 11:19 Psa 25:4 Jer 2:33, אֶת־הָרָעֹות לְמּדְתּי אֶת־דְּרָכַיִךְ “wickedness hast thou taught thy ways;” Jer 13:21, לִמַּדְתְּי אֹתָם עָלַיִךְ אַלֻּפִים “thou hast taught them to be leaders over thee,” thou hast accustomed them to exercise dominion over thee, Jer 9:4, 13 Ecc 12:9.-(d) followed by an acc. of pers. and dat. of thing (prop. to train some one to some thing), Psa 18:35 144:1 followed by בְּ (to train in something), Isa 40:14 מִן of the thing, Psa 94:12 by a gerund, Psa 143:10.-(e) followed by a dat. of pers. Job 21:22.

Pual, to be accustomed, to be trained, used of a calf, Hos 10:11 of soldiers (see Kal No. 1). Son 3:8, מְלֻמְּדֵי מִלְחָמָה “trained for war.” Comp. 1Ch 25:7, מְלֻמְּדֵי שִׁיר “instructed in singing.” Used of the thing to which any one is trained. Isa 29:13, מִצְוַת אֲנָשִׁים מְלֻמָּדָה “a human commandment taught (to men);” that which they are made to learn.

Derivatives, לִמּוּד, מַלְמָד, תַּלִמִיד.
